aliases: 青菠萝蜜咖喱 (Qīng Bōluómì Gālí)
कच्चे कटहल की सब्जी (Kaccē Kathal kī Sabzī)

chinese: 青菠萝蜜咖喱 (Qīng Bōluómì Gālí)
hindi: कच्चे कटहल की सब्जी (Kaccē Kathal kī Sabzī)

region_of_origin: South and Southeast Asia (India, Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia)
food_subgroup: Cooked young/unripe jackfruit in spiced curry sauce; used as a vegetable side dish or plant-based meat-texture substitute

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus) as a food entity but does not have a specific term for 'young green jackfruit curry'. Closest parent used; the curry preparation and unripe-stage specificity are not captured in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No specific FoodEx2 code identified for young green jackfruit curry. FoodEx2 covers jackfruit as a raw commodity but not this specific curry preparation. Null retained pending expert mapping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for young green jackfruit curry. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual curry preparations at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of young green jackfruit curry is approximately 200g, representing a typical side-dish portion of jackfruit pieces in curry sauce as served at a Malay nasi padang stall or Indian vegetarian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Young Green Jackfruit — FAO/INFOODS South-East Asian Food Composition Table (SEA FCT) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Jackfruit, raw, FNDDS)
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS South-East Asian Food Composition Table; USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for young/unripe jackfruit are drawn from FAO/INFOODS SEA FCT (primary regional source) and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry for raw jackfruit (FDC ID 174687). Values reflect the cooked curry dish (jackfruit + curry sauce) at approximately 200g per serving. Sodium is elevated relative to raw jackfruit due to curry seasoning. Fat reflects added cooking oil and/or coconut milk in the curry base. Values are consistent with HPB Singapore nutrient data for vegetable curry dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Young/Unripe Jackfruit — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature on unripe jackfruit starch
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~35 (low) for young/unripe jackfruit is referenced from University of Sydney GI Database data for unripe jackfruit. Before ripening, jackfruit starch is predominantly complex and resistant, yielding a low GI. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 13.0g) / 100 = 4.55, rounded to 4.6. Serving carbohydrate = 6.5g/100g × 200g = 13.0g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 180mg/100g (360mg per 200g serving) from curry seasoning; partially offset by potassium 220mg/100g (440mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Young green jackfruit curry provides moderate sodium (360mg per serving) typical of a curry dish; patients with hypertension should monitor total daily sodium intake and request reduced-salt preparation where possible, while benefiting from the potassium content (440mg per serving) which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~35 (low), GL ~4.6 per 200g serving; complex unripened starch with minimal sugar (1.5g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Young green jackfruit curry has an excellent glycaemic profile (GI 35, GL 4.6 per serving) making it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management; however, the very low protein content (3.4g per 200g serving) means it must be paired with a genuine protein source (dal, tofu, tempeh, legumes) at the same meal to achieve satiety and prevent compensatory carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.8g/100g), zero cholesterol, zero trans fat; moderate sodium from curry seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Young green jackfruit curry is low in saturated fat (1.6g per serving) and contains no cholesterol or trans fat, making it a heart-friendly vegetable dish; sodium at 360mg per serving is moderate and should be factored into daily sodium limits for cardiovascular patients, and the dish should be paired with adequate protein sources to support overall cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat, anti-inflammatory spice base; very low protein (3.4g per 200g serving) is a concern for muscle mass preservation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Young green jackfruit curry is low in saturated fat and contains anti-inflammatory spices, but its very low protein content (3.4g per 200g serving) is a significant concern for rheumatoid arthritis patients who require adequate protein to preserve muscle mass during chronic inflammation; always pair with a high-protein food at the same meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Young green jackfruit curry is a Southeast Asian and South Asian vegetable dish made from unripe jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus) — the fibrous, pale, starchy flesh of the jackfruit harvested before it ripens and becomes sweet. When simmered in a spiced curry sauce (typically containing onion, garlic, ginger, chilli, turmeric, and coconut milk or water), the jackfruit shreds into fibrous strands that closely resemble pulled pork or shredded chicken in texture, giving it widespread appeal as a plant-based meat-texture substitute. Nutritionally, however, young jackfruit is a vegetable, not a protein food: it contains only 1.7g protein per 100g (3.4g per 200g serving), compared to chicken breast at approximately 31g/100g. CRITICAL CLINICAL ALERT: Young jackfruit is increasingly marketed as a 'plant-based protein' or 'pulled pork substitute' — this framing is nutritionally misleading. It can substitute meat's texture but not its protein content. Patients who replace chicken or fish with young jackfruit as their primary protein source will be severely protein-deficient. Always ensure jackfruit dishes are paired with genuine plant protein sources such as dal, tempeh, tofu, or legumes. The glycaemic index is very low (~35) due to the complex, resistant, unripened starch, making it excellent for Type 2 Diabetes management from a glycaemic perspective (GL ~4.6 per 200g serving). Sodium is moderate at 180mg/100g (360mg per serving) from curry seasoning. The dish is naturally vegan, vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. In Singapore, it is available as kari nangka at Malay nasi padang stalls and Indian vegetarian restaurants, and as canned young jackfruit (brands: Chaokoh, Golden Circle) at supermarkets. Key nutritional distinction: YOUNG/UNRIPE jackfruit (this record) is fundamentally different from RIPE jackfruit — unripe: low sugar (~1.5g/100g), low GI (~35), used as a vegetable; ripe: high sugar (~23g/100g), high GI (~75), classified as a fruit.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values for young green jackfruit curry are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA FCT and USDA SR Legacy for raw jackfruit, adjusted for curry preparation (added fat, sodium from seasoning). No exact match for this specific dish exists in a single approved primary source. GI is referenced from University of Sydney GI Database for unripe jackfruit as an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for young green jackfruit curry are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA FCT or USDA SR Legacy at the dish level as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the cooked curry dish is not reported in the primary sources consulted. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats). cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 (naturally zero — entirely plant-based, no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed 0.0 (naturally zero — no UV-exposed mushrooms or animal sources). CRITICAL PROTEIN WARNING: Young jackfruit is very low in protein (~1.7g/100g, 3.4g per 200g serving) and cannot function as a protein substitute for meat. Patients and clinicians should be aware that marketing of young jackfruit as a 'plant-based protein' is nutritionally misleading. Nutritional distinction: this record covers YOUNG/UNRIPE jackfruit curry only; ripe jackfruit has a fundamentally different nutritional profile (high sugar ~23g/100g, high GI ~75) and is not covered by this record.
